sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
The cortex ontology is defined in `cortex_ontology.yaml` at the project root. It specifies all valid node types, edge types, and validation rules. The builder enforces these rules at build time â€” invalid edges fail the build.

The ontology is based on PROV-O (W3C Provenance Ontology) principles with a process-centric model: all code is a series of processes, and the node type describes the process role, not the language construct.

sections.heading: Node Types
sections.content:
### atomic_process
A leaf-level transformation unit with no sub-processes. The smallest meaningful unit of work.
- Examples: Python function, dFlow `@task`, single pipeline step
- Can contain: nothing
- dFlow mapping: `@task` decorated function

### composite_process
Orchestrates or contains other processes. Scale-invariant: a module, package, pipeline, and `@workflow` are all composite_process.
- Examples: Python module, package, dFlow `@workflow`, Snakemake pipeline
- Can contain: `atomic_process`, `composite_process`
- dFlow mapping: `@workflow` decorated function, any module/package

### entity
Something consumed or produced by a process.
- Subtypes: `data_artifact`, `config_artifact`, `model_artifact`, `external_package`
- Examples: AnnData object, config.yaml, trained model, third-party pip package
- dFlow mapping: `inputs=`/`outputs=` values on decorators (best-effort)

### constraint
Restricts or validates behavior of processes or entities.
- Subtypes: `test`, `decision`, `schema`, `contract`
- Examples: `@test_workflow` function, `@test_suite` class, design decision
- dFlow mapping: `@test_workflow` and `@test_suite` decorated items

### document
Human-readable description of any other node.
- Examples: Markdown file, README, DocJSON file, notebook
- Can contain: `document` (sections within documents)

sections.heading: Edge Types
sections.content:
| Edge Type | From Types | To Types | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `consumes` | atomic_process, composite_process | entity | Process takes entity as input |
| `produces` | atomic_process, composite_process | entity | Process creates entity as output |
| `composes` | composite_process, atomic_process | atomic_process, composite_process | Structural containment (moduleâ†’function) |
| `delegates_to` | composite_process | atomic_process, composite_process | Runtime invocation (cross-module call) |
| `depends_on` | atomic_process, composite_process | atomic_process, composite_process, entity | Execution/import dependency |
| `constrains` | constraint | atomic_process, composite_process, entity | Schema/contract restriction |
| `validates` | atomic_process, constraint | atomic_process, composite_process | Test coverage link |
| `documents` | document | any | Documentation describes a node |
| `supersedes` | atomic_process, composite_process, constraint | same types | Replacement/obsolescence |

sections.heading: Validation Rules
sections.content:
Enforced by `cortex/index/builder.py` at build time:

| Rule | Level | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `require_valid_node_type` | Error | Every node must have a node_type from the ontology |
| `require_valid_edge_type` | Error | Every edge must have an edge_type from the ontology |
| `require_valid_edge_endpoints` | Error | Edge from/to node types must match the edge_type's allowed types |
| `warn_empty_composite` | Warning | composite_process with no outgoing composes/delegates_to edges |
| `report_untested_atomic` | Info | atomic_process with no inbound validates edge (untested function) |

Edge validation is also enforced at runtime by `cortex link` via `cortex/ontology.py::validate_edge()`.
